# Copilot Instructions — Astro SEO Project

## 🧭 Project Overview

This is an **Astro** project optimized for maximum search engine visibility, Core Web Vitals performance, and structured discoverability. Every decision — from component structure to content modeling — must serve organic growth and indexability.

---

## 🏗️ Tech Stack

- **Framework**: [Astro](https://astro.build) (SSG/SSR hybrid as needed)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS
- **Content**: Astro Content Collections (`.md` / `.mdx`)
- **Deployment**: Vercel / Netlify (edge-compatible)
- **Analytics**: Google Search Console + GA4 (or Plausible)
- **Image optimization**: `astro:assets` + `<Image />` component

---

## 📁 Project Structure

```
src/
├── components/
│   ├── seo/              # SEO-specific components (Head, Schema, OG)
│   ├── ui/               # Reusable UI components
│   └── layout/           # Page wrappers
├── content/
│   ├── blog/             # Blog posts (.mdx)
│   ├── pages/            # Static content pages
│   └── config.ts         # Collection schemas (Zod)
├── layouts/
│   └── BaseLayout.astro   # Main layout with full SEO head
├── pages/
│   ├── index.astro
│   ├── blog/
│   │   ├── index.astro
│   │   └── [slug].astro
│   └── [...slug].astro   # Dynamic catch-all
├── utils/
│   └── seo.ts            # SEO helpers (canonical, OG, schema builders)
public/
├── robots.txt
├── sitemap-index.xml     # Auto-generated by @astrojs/sitemap
└── favicon.svg
```

---

## 🔧 Required Integrations

Always install and configure these integrations:

```ts
// astro.config.mjs
import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
import sitemap from "@astrojs/sitemap";
import tailwind from "@astrojs/tailwind";
import mdx from "@astrojs/mdx";

export default defineConfig({
  site: "https://yourdomain.com", // REQUIRED for sitemap and canonicals
  integrations: [
    sitemap({
      changefreq: "weekly",
      priority: 0.7,
      lastmod: new Date(),
    }),
    tailwind(),
    mdx(),
  ],
  output: "static", // or 'hybrid' for SSR pages
});
```

---

## 🧠 SEO Rules — Always Follow

### 1. Every page MUST have a `<SEOHead />` component

Create `src/components/seo/SEOHead.astro`:

```astro
---
export interface Props {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  canonical?: string;
  ogImage?: string;
  ogType?: 'website' | 'article';
  noindex?: boolean;
  publishedDate?: string;
  modifiedDate?: string;
  author?: string;
}

const {
  title,
  description,
  canonical = Astro.url.href,
  ogImage = '/og-default.png',
  ogType = 'website',
  noindex = false,
  publishedDate,
  modifiedDate,
  author,
} = Astro.props;

const siteName = 'Your Site Name';
const fullTitle = `${title} | ${siteName}`;
---

<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />

  <!-- Primary SEO -->
  <title>{fullTitle}</title>
  <meta name="description" content={description} />
  <link rel="canonical" href={canonical} />
  {noindex && <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow" />}

  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:title" content={fullTitle} />
  <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
  <meta property="og:url" content={canonical} />
  <meta property="og:type" content={ogType} />
  <meta property="og:image" content={new URL(ogImage, Astro.site)} />
  <meta property="og:site_name" content={siteName} />
  <meta property="og:locale" content="es_CO" />

  <!-- Twitter Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
  <meta name="twitter:title" content={fullTitle} />
  <meta name="twitter:description" content={description} />
  <meta name="twitter:image" content={new URL(ogImage, Astro.site)} />

  <!-- Article metadata -->
  {publishedDate && <meta property="article:published_time" content={publishedDate} />}
  {modifiedDate && <meta property="article:modified_time" content={modifiedDate} />}
  {author && <meta property="article:author" content={author} />}

  <!-- Favicons -->
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/apple-touch-icon.png" />

  <!-- Sitemap discovery -->
  <link rel="sitemap" href="/sitemap-index.xml" />
</head>
```

---

### 2. Structured Data (JSON-LD) — Always inject per page type

Create `src/components/seo/SchemaOrg.astro`:

```astro
---
export interface Props {
  schema: Record<string, unknown> | Record<string, unknown>[];
}
const { schema } = Astro.props;
const jsonld = JSON.stringify(Array.isArray(schema) ? schema : [schema]);
---
<script type="application/ld+json" set:html={jsonld} />
```

**Schema templates to use by page type:**

```ts
// src/utils/seo.ts

export const websiteSchema = (url: string, name: string) => ({
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "WebSite",
  url,
  name,
  potentialAction: {
    "@type": "SearchAction",
    target: `${url}/search?q={search_term_string}`,
    "query-input": "required name=search_term_string",
  },
});

export const articleSchema = ({
  title,
  description,
  url,
  image,
  datePublished,
  dateModified,
  authorName,
  publisherName,
  publisherLogo,
}: {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  url: string;
  image: string;
  datePublished: string;
  dateModified: string;
  authorName: string;
  publisherName: string;
  publisherLogo: string;
}) => ({
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Article",
  headline: title,
  description,
  url,
  image,
  datePublished,
  dateModified,
  author: { "@type": "Person", name: authorName },
  publisher: {
    "@type": "Organization",
    name: publisherName,
    logo: { "@type": "ImageObject", url: publisherLogo },
  },
});

export const breadcrumbSchema = (items: { name: string; url: string }[]) => ({
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BreadcrumbList",
  itemListElement: items.map((item, i) => ({
    "@type": "ListItem",
    position: i + 1,
    name: item.name,
    item: item.url,
  })),
});

export const faqSchema = (faqs: { question: string; answer: string }[]) => ({
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  mainEntity: faqs.map(({ question, answer }) => ({
    "@type": "Question",
    name: question,
    acceptedAnswer: { "@type": "Answer", text: answer },
  })),
});

export const localBusinessSchema = ({
  name,
  description,
  url,
  image,
  telephone,
  address,
}: {
  name: string;
  description: string;
  url: string;
  image: string;
  telephone: string;
  address: {
    street: string;
    city: string;
    region: string;
    country: string;
    postal: string;
  };
}) => ({
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LocalBusiness",
  name,
  description,
  url,
  image,
  telephone,
  address: {
    "@type": "PostalAddress",
    streetAddress: address.street,
    addressLocality: address.city,
    addressRegion: address.region,
    addressCountry: address.country,
    postalCode: address.postal,
  },
});
```

---

### 3. Content Collections — Always define Zod schemas with SEO fields

```ts
// src/content/config.ts
import { defineCollection, z } from "astro:content";

const blogCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string().max(60), // Title tag limit
    description: z.string().min(120).max(160), // Meta description limit
    publishedDate: z.coerce.date(),
    modifiedDate: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    author: z.string().default("Editorial Team"),
    ogImage: z.string().optional(),
    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    category: z.string(),
    noindex: z.boolean().default(false),
    canonical: z.string().url().optional(), // For cross-published content
    focusKeyword: z.string().optional(), // Primary keyword for this post
    relatedPosts: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // slugs
  }),
});

export const collections = { blog: blogCollection };
```

---

### 4. robots.txt — Always maintain

```txt
# public/robots.txt
User-agent: *
Allow: /

Disallow: /api/
Disallow: /_astro/
Disallow: /admin/

Sitemap: https://yourdomain.com/sitemap-index.xml
```

---

### 5. Image Optimization — Always use `<Image />`

```astro
---
import { Image } from 'astro:assets';
---

<!-- ALWAYS: use Image component with alt, width, height -->
<Image
  src={import('../assets/hero.jpg')}
  alt="Descriptive alt text with keyword"
  width={1200}
  height={630}
  format="webp"
  quality={80}
  loading="eager"  <!-- For above-the-fold images -->
/>

<!-- Below the fold: loading="lazy" -->
```

**Rules:**

- Every `<img>` must have a meaningful `alt` attribute (not empty, not "image")
- Use `loading="eager"` only for LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) images
- Prefer WebP format
- Never use `width: 100%` without explicit `width`/`height` to avoid CLS

---

### 6. Internal Linking Strategy

- Every blog post must link to at least **2–3 internal pages**
- Use descriptive anchor text (never "click here" or "read more")
- Create a `RelatedPosts` component that renders automatically from `relatedPosts` frontmatter

```astro
---
// src/components/ui/RelatedPosts.astro
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';

const { slugs } = Astro.props;
const allPosts = await getCollection('blog');
const related = allPosts.filter(p => slugs.includes(p.slug));
---
<section aria-label="Artículos relacionados">
  <h2>También te puede interesar</h2>
  {related.map(post => (
    <a href={`/blog/${post.slug}`}>{post.data.title}</a>
  ))}
</section>
```

---

### 7. Heading Hierarchy — Strict Rules

- **One `<h1>` per page**, always. Never skip levels (h1 → h3).
- `<h1>` must contain the primary keyword naturally
- `<h2>` for main sections, `<h3>` for subsections
- Never use headings for styling; use CSS classes instead

---

### 8. Core Web Vitals — Performance Rules

**LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) < 2.5s:**

- Preload hero images: `<link rel="preload" as="image" href="/hero.webp" />`
- Use `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"` on above-fold images
- Avoid render-blocking fonts; use `font-display: swap`

**CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1:**

- Always set `width` + `height` on images and videos
- Reserve space for dynamic content (ads, embeds)
- Avoid inserting content above existing content

**INP (Interaction to Next Paint) < 200ms:**

- Minimize client-side JS. Use Astro islands only when necessary
- Defer non-critical scripts with `is:inline` + `defer`
- Never block the main thread with heavy parsing

```astro
<!-- Fonts: non-blocking -->
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
<link rel="preload" as="style" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=YourFont&display=swap" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=YourFont&display=swap" media="print" onload="this.media='all'" />
```

---

### 9. URL Structure Rules

- URLs must be **lowercase**, **hyphen-separated**, **descriptive**
- Include target keyword in the URL when natural
- Avoid dates in URLs (e.g., `/blog/2024/01/title`) — use `/blog/slug`
- Max URL depth: 3 levels (`/category/subcategory/article`)
- Avoid query strings for indexable content

---

### 10. Pagination — SEO-safe

```astro
---
// Paginate with rel="next" / rel="prev"
const { page } = Astro.props;
---
{page.url.prev && <link rel="prev" href={page.url.prev} />}
{page.url.next && <link rel="next" href={page.url.next} />}

<!-- Canonical on page 2+ points to page 1 -->
<link rel="canonical" href={page.currentPage > 1 ? page.url.first : Astro.url.href} />
```

---

## 📝 Content Guidelines

When generating or suggesting content:

1. **Title tags**: 50–60 characters, keyword near the beginning
2. **Meta descriptions**: 120–160 characters, include CTA, unique per page
3. **Content length**: Minimum 800 words for blog posts targeting competitive keywords
4. **Keyword density**: 1–2% naturally distributed; avoid stuffing
5. **Semantic HTML**: Use `<article>`, `<section>`, `<nav>`, `<aside>`, `<main>`, `<header>`, `<footer>`
6. **Featured snippet optimization**: Use question-based H2s + concise answer in the first paragraph
7. **Alt text**: Describes the image content AND includes keyword when relevant

---

## 🚀 Checklist Before Every Page

Before considering a page complete, verify:

- [ ] `<SEOHead />` present with title, description, canonical, ogImage
- [ ] One `<h1>` with primary keyword
- [ ] JSON-LD schema appropriate for page type
- [ ] All images have `alt`, `width`, `height`
- [ ] Internal links to 2–3 relevant pages
- [ ] Meta description is 120–160 chars
- [ ] URL is clean and keyword-friendly
- [ ] No duplicate content with existing pages
- [ ] Breadcrumb component present (for non-homepage)
- [ ] Reading time calculated and displayed (for blog posts)

---

## 🛠️ Utility Functions

```ts
// src/utils/seo.ts (additions)

/** Calculate reading time from markdown content */
export function readingTime(content: string): string {
  const words = content.trim().split(/\s+/).length;
  const minutes = Math.ceil(words / 200);
  return `${minutes} min de lectura`;
}

/** Build absolute URL */
export function absoluteUrl(path: string, site: string): string {
  return new URL(path, site).href;
}

/** Truncate meta description safely */
export function truncateDescription(text: string, max = 160): string {
  if (text.length <= max) return text;
  return text.slice(0, max - 3).trimEnd() + "...";
}

/** Generate OG image URL (for dynamic OG with Satori or Cloudinary) */
export function ogImageUrl(title: string, site: string): string {
  const params = new URLSearchParams({ title });
  return `${site}/api/og?${params.toString()}`;
}
```

---

## 🗺️ Dynamic Sitemap Priorities

```ts
// astro.config.mjs - custom sitemap priority per route
sitemap({
  customPages: ['https://yourdomain.com/landing-especial'],
  serialize(item) {
    if (item.url.includes('/blog/')) {
      return { ...item, priority: 0.8, changefreq: 'monthly' };
    }
    if (item.url === 'https://yourdomain.com/') {
      return { ...item, priority: 1.0, changefreq: 'daily' };
    }
    return { ...item, priority: 0.6 };
  },
}),
```

---

## ⚠️ Anti-patterns — Never Do This

- ❌ Duplicate `<title>` or `<meta name="description">` tags
- ❌ Pages without a canonical URL
- ❌ `noindex` on pages that should rank
- ❌ Lazy-loading the LCP image
- ❌ Heading tags used only for visual sizing
- ❌ Missing `alt` on informational images
- ❌ JavaScript-rendered content without SSR/prerendering (Googlebot may miss it)
- ❌ Blocking CSS/JS in `<head>` without async/defer
- ❌ Multiple H1s on a single page
- ❌ Generic anchor text ("haz clic aquí", "ver más")
- ❌ Thin content pages under 300 words indexed
- ❌ URL parameters indexed (use `?` only for non-indexable state)

---

## 📊 Monitoring Integrations

Always include in `BaseLayout.astro`:

```astro
<!-- Google Search Console verification -->
<meta name="google-site-verification" content="YOUR_CODE" />

<!-- GA4 (non-blocking) -->
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=G-XXXXXXXX" is:inline></script>
```

Configure in environment variables:

```
PUBLIC_GA4_ID=G-XXXXXXXX
PUBLIC_SITE_URL=https://yourdomain.com
PUBLIC_SITE_NAME="Your Site Name"
```

---

_These instructions are the source of truth for all SEO-related decisions in this project. When in doubt: prefer indexability, performance, and semantic correctness over visual complexity._
